what_it_does: This file is a research synthesis document for the Self-Serve Core scope (scope C) of the company project. It consolidates findings from a parallel read-only exploration, states a one-line verdict that roughly 80% of the substrate already exists, decomposes the desired self-serve capabilities, lists per-subsystem round 1 findings with existing implementations and gaps, synthesizes the real new work into five grouped workstreams (C1-C5), notes coordination with recollection/wildcard/consumers, and records resolved and open questions for the next round.
observations: Declares itself as the EVIDENCE BASE and ground truth; completion criteria and implementation guide must flow from it, never reverse.
Verdict: ~80% already exists; the build is assembly plus a few real new pieces, not from scratch.

Decomposes self-serve into era clones, channels/rules/guides/project-info, session-history search, session storage/project hierarchy, attachments/types, MCP surface, and onboarding/supervisor/frontend/design.
Identifies specific gaps per subsystem with file and line references (e.g., cc_clone.py:145, session_recall.py:337).
C1 search hierarchy adopts a freshness-not-scope boundary: recollection owns deep-corpus recall; session_recall owns the live edge; the complete recall is a union at the freshness seam.
C2-C5 group the real new work into onboarding/discoverability, self-serve gap closures, MCP coherence, and frontend exposure.
Coordination section emphasizes consumers (recollection, wildcard, interface/core-ops, future sessions) and explicitly avoids parallel systems.
Open question resolved on cross-project search ownership; remaining questions concern channel rules representation and onboarding delivery mechanism.
standouts: The freshness-not-scope architectural decision resolving Company versus recollection search ownership.
The prime directive: do not spec parallel systems — extend what is here.
The ~80% exists verdict reframes the project as assembly rather than greenfield.
The supervisor mailbox plus at-point launch are identified as the onboarding-delivery substrate.
conventions: Uses markdown headers and bullet lists.
Cites code files with line numbers for claims.
Labels gaps with bold uppercase GAPS sections.
Uses shorthand identifiers like C1-C5 for workstreams.
Includes a metadata header with loop-prep round, date, scope, and provenance.
Treats synthesis as ground truth that downstream artifacts must follow.
concerns: Heavy reliance on line-number citations that may stale quickly.
Strong prescriptive conclusions (never reverse, prime directive) embedded in a research synthesis.
Some gaps framed as resolved by assertion without showing evidence in this file (e.g., recollection's backfill driver status).
Mixes findings, architectural decisions, and implementation sequencing in one document, which could blur evidence versus opinion.
notes: A research synthesis that maps existing substrate and gaps for a self-serve core build, then turns the findings into five sequenced workstreams and coordination rules.
questions: What exactly is a session versus project versus channel in this domain?
What does freshness seam mean operationally in terms of merge and deduplication logic?
How is project derived or stamped when cwd is the primary key?
What is the relationship between cc_channels.py and session_channels.py, and why are there two channel systems?
What design-lint rules does the existing design system enforce?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be an evidence-based research synthesis that feeds completion criteria and an implementation guide. Actual content matches: it reports findings, identifies gaps, and derives a build shape, though it also encodes architectural decisions and sequencing that go beyond pure synthesis.
apparent_themes: Self-serve discoverability for fresh and spawned sessions.
Cross-system coordination (Company versus recollection versus wildcard).
intention_signals: The file is trying to align a multi-system build around an existing substrate, prevent duplicate cross-project search engines, and package the remaining work into a coherent, consumer-aware implementation plan.
novelty: The freshness-not-scope framing and explicit union of live-edge (session_recall) with deep-corpus (recollection) at a freshness seam, plus treating onboarding as deliverable via the supervisor mailbox and spawn substrate.
contribution: This file contributes a consolidated gap analysis and a proposed grouping and sequencing of workstreams that a fused system could keep as a planning artifact, especially the architectural boundary between live-edge and deep-corpus recall and the directive to extend rather than duplicate existing substrate.
summary_for_embedding: Research synthesis for the company self-serve core scope, assessing existing substrate across era clones, channels, session search, storage, attachments, MCP surface, and onboarding/frontend, identifying gaps, and proposing five workstreams built on a freshness-not-scope search boundary.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the ground-truth evidence base that shapes completion criteria and an implementation guide for assembling a self-serve session fabric without parallel systems.
